fsExpectations: history line 1 has source=upload, sha256=abc123..., version=1

when: POST upload with original content (sha=A) — currentSha is B

fsExpectations: incoming/.history/合約 (2).docx.jsonl has 1 line, source=upload, sha256=A
incoming/.history/合約.docx.jsonl now has 3 lines, last with source=upload-conflict-rename, sha256=A, redirectedTo='合約 (2).docx'

responseExpectations.body.error: no session project path; refusing to fall back to legacy attachment cache

fsExpectations: no file written under any incoming/
no AttachmentBlob row written to session storage
incoming/.history/合約.docx.jsonl last line has source=bundle-published
behaviorExpectations: docxmcp container received tools/call docx_decompose with input=/state/staging/A.docx
Bus event mcp.dispatcher.cache-miss emitted with {appId: docxmcp, sha256: A}

preExistingState.bundlesAt: ~/.local/state/opencode/mcp-staging/docxmcp/bundles/A/ (populated by an earlier session in proj-a)

when: AI tool call docx_decompose(input='incoming/重要文件.docx') in proj-b

behaviorExpectations: docxmcp container received NO tools/call (verified via mock or container log absence)
Bus event mcp.dispatcher.cache-hit emitted with {appId: docxmcp, sha256: A}
fsExpectations: stat(/tmp/opencode-test/proj-b/incoming/重要文件/description.md).st_nlink == 3 (cache + proj-a publish + proj-b publish)

when: AI tool call Write(path='incoming/紀錄.md', content='new...')

fsExpectations: history line 2 has source=tool:Write, sha256=Y, sessionId=<test-session>

given.externalActionBeforeLookup: shell `echo new > incoming/合約.docx` — mtime now 2000, size 4, sha=C

fsExpectations: history line 2 has source=drift-detected, sha256=C, mtime=2000, sizeBytes=4, sessionId=null

when: Inspect docker run command actually used

behaviorExpectations: command contains '-v ~/.local/state/opencode/mcp-staging/docxmcp/:/state' (or equivalent)
command does NOT contain '-v /tmp/opencode-test/proj-a' or any prefix of $HOME outside the staging area

when: POST upload, then `git status --porcelain` in proj-a-git

shellExpectations: git status output contains '?? incoming/合約.docx' (untracked)
no `git add` command was executed by daemon (verified via shell command audit log)

sessionStorageRow.content: <Uint8Array of 12345 bytes — base64 envelope in LegacyStore>

behaviorExpectations: row has no repoPath; backend takes legacy path
returned AttachmentBlob.content is the original Uint8Array of 12345 bytes
no fs read attempted
row has repoPath; backend reads bytes from <projectRoot>/incoming/合約.docx
returned AttachmentBlob.content matches file bytes (sha256 matches stored sha256)
no legacy base64 decode attempted

when: getAttachmentBlob — incoming/missing.docx does not exist on disk

behaviorExpectations: throws attachment_repo_file_missing (INC-3001')
does NOT fall back to checking content field (no-silent-fallback rule)

given.preCheck: stat(both paths).st_ino must be equal (verifying hard-link)

when: AI tool call Edit(path='incoming/合約/description.md', oldContent=..., newContent=...)

fsExpectations: cache content unchanged (sha256 of cache file == sha256 from manifest.json)
incoming/.history/heavy.txt.jsonl exists with exactly 1 line (the new entry)
incoming/重要報告.docx exists (control chars stripped)
history line 1 annotation contains the original raw filename for audit

preExistingState.bundlesAt: ~/.local/state/opencode/mcp-staging/docxmcp/bundles/A/ (on home fs, populated by earlier session)

when: AI tool call docx_decompose(input='incoming/合約.docx') in proj-x — cache-hit path

behaviorExpectations: dispatcher detects stat(<repo>).st_dev != stat(<staging>).st_dev
dispatcher uses cp -r instead of link()
Bus event mcp.dispatcher.cross-fs-fallback emitted with {appId: docxmcp, sha256: A, reason: 'diff-st_dev'}
fsExpectations: /mnt/external/proj-x/incoming/合約/description.md exists with full content (cp success)

preExistingState.comment: manifest sha intentionally != directory sha (corrupted state)

behaviorExpectations: dispatcher reads bundles/A/manifest.json, detects sha mismatch (A != BBBBB)
Bus event mcp.dispatcher.cache-corrupted emitted with {appId: docxmcp, sha256: A, expectedSha: A, actualSha: BBBBB}
dispatcher falls through to cache-miss path: stage + invoke mcp tool + publish
docxmcp container DID receive tools/call (verified via mock or container log)
fsExpectations: bundles/A/manifest.json now has sha256=A (overwritten by recompute)

when: dispatcher.after rewrites the result before passing to LLM

behaviorExpectations: all occurrences of '/state/bundles/A/' replaced with 'incoming/合約/'
no occurrences of staging host absolute path leaked in result string
non-path strings inside the result body are untouched (audit a control string with '/state/foo' that isn't a real path — it should remain because it doesn't match the registered prefix exactly)

when: Test code uses direct `fs.writeFile('incoming/合約/description.md', 'tampered')` bypassing all hooks

fsExpectations: stat(both paths).st_ino still equal (hard-link not broken)
cache file content is now 'tampered' — confirming a tool that bypasses break-on-write WILL pollute cache
this test is intentionally a witness: any future tool that touches incoming must go through dispatcher.breakHardLinkBeforeWrite
then.interpretation: PASS = the negative test correctly demonstrates the rule's necessity. FAIL would mean break-on-write somehow worked through fs syscalls (impossible) — would indicate test setup bug.
